title: Revert opening load sequence and add controls to start screen

requirements.summary: Revert InitialLoadingScreen to original Bond-themed gun barrel animation with original timing
acceptanceCriteria: InitialLoadingScreen displays the original Bond-themed gun barrel animation without extended timing modifications
The loading screen minimum display time is restored to its original value (not the extended 007 intro duration from recent versions)
All original scan-line overlay, progress bar, and corner accent decorations are present
The transition from the loading screen to the start/mission page matches the original behavior

file_operations.description: Restore the original minimum display time from 4.5 seconds to the intended baseline (if 4.5s is already the extended version, revert to the shorter original timing). Remove any extended duration changes introduced in recent versions. Ensure the Bond-themed gun barrel animation, scan-line overlay, progress bar, and corner accent decorations all use their original timing values and visual properties.

requirements.summary: Add controls reference panel to the start/mission screen
acceptanceCriteria: The start/mission screen displays a clearly labeled controls section listing all player keybindings (WASD, mouse look, shoot, space)
The controls panel is styled consistently with the existing James Bond / Die Hard black, charcoal, and gold theme
The controls panel does not obstruct the 'Begin Mission' call-to-action button
Controls are readable at desktop resolutions and do not break the layout

file_operations.description: Add a controls reference panel to the start/mission screen UI that appears before the game begins. Display a clearly labeled section listing all player keybindings: WASD for movement, mouse for aim and shoot, Space for jump/interact, and any other relevant controls from the usePlayerControls hook. Style the panel using the existing James Bond / Die Hard black, charcoal, and gold theme variables. Position the panel so it does not obstruct the 'Begin Mission' button and remains readable at desktop resolutions. The panel should only appear on the start screen, not during active gameplay.
